a:r.a:t: Telemarketing | Maturity assessment
Topic
Status
HGV Observations
Initial Recommendations
Eligibility
All telemarketing roles on a sales compensation plans meet the three eligibility criteria
N/A – All roles on plans meet standard eligibility criteria
Target Cash Compensation
Telemarketing roles are generally well-aligned to direct competitors and vacation ownership survey data
Manager+ levels generally align to the 25
th
market percentile of broader industry benchmarks, which may impact external talent attraction, particularly for higher levels
While TCC is generally competitive with the market, shifting more towards market median or above for Manager+ roles may assist with recruiting from outside the vacation ownership industry
Pay Mix & Leverage
The pay mix for the agent-level roles is generally aligned with market practice
The pay mix for manager and director roles are more heavily weighted towards variable compensation then we typically see in the market
Consider shifting the pay mix for the management roles more towards base salary to align to market practice and to align manager focus to business strategy and coaching/development
Measures & Weights
Comp plans usually have 2-4 measures, but some have 5 or 6.
Most measures make up 20% or more of target variable compensation; however, some roles have multiple measures at 10-15% of variable compensation.
There are up to 8 performance tiers for some roles, which is higher than market norm
The selected measures appear to be outcome oriented and are appropriate for their role.
Consider consolidating / reducing the number of measures of plans with more than 4 measures.
Consider if measures making up 10-15% of variable comp are actually needed.
Simplify payout curves and reduce the number of tiers
Compensation Ranges
Currently unable to assess with the data received
Unable to fully assess compensation ranges with the data provided at this time
Payment Periods
Telemarketing comp plan periods are mostly set to monthly cycles which aligns to the high volume and short sales cycle focus of the industry
N/A – Payment cycles generally align to market practice
Quota Setting
Monthly targets are set on a quarterly basis based on budgeted call volume
Potential candidates express that HGV targets are higher than competitors
Unable to fully assess quota setting with the data provided at this time, though we are hearing that HGV targets may be higher than competitors
SPIFFs
HGV has formal SPIFF policy guardrails, including approval and payroll processing requirements and are used as supplemental short-term incentives (site-specific)
Continue using SPIFFs selectively for targeted short-term behavior change
Strengthen governance and tracking to ensure SPIFF usage remains controlled and aligned to business priorities across all sites
Telemarketing
Marketing
Sales
Limited Issues / Minor Opportunities for Improvement
Moderate Issues / Clear Opportunity for Improvement
Significant Issues Discovered / Action Required to Address Gaps
Insufficient Data Available
Compensation Design & Market Assessment
